newsSinceJun6: No AI-chain-relevant news. Recent flow is residential-building-products driven: a Seeking Alpha contributor upgrade to Strong Buy citing OmniMax acquisition synergies (synergy target raised to $26M total, $16M in FY26 adjusted EBITDA) and valuation support after the ~22% YTD drawdown; management (CEO Bosway, CFO Lovechio) presents at Wells Fargo's 16th Annual Industrial & Materials Conference on June 10. FY26 guidance reaffirmed ($1.76-1.83B sales, $310-326M adj. EBITDA). Today's +2.33% looks like pre-conference drift in a beaten-down small cap, not a fundamental AI-related event.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - OmniMax-driven analyst upgrade and investor meetings
Seeking Alpha - Even As Conditions Worsen, This Play Deserves An Upgrade
GuruFocus - ROCK to discuss strategic developments at upcoming conference (Wells Fargo, June 10)

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing since June 6 strengthens or weakens the latent AI-node pathway; all incremental news (OmniMax synergies, contributor upgrade, Wells Fargo conference) is residential roofing/building products, orthogonal to the strict screen's repurposing thesis. Yesterday's hold stands and today's +2.3% is immaterial drift, not a setup change.
verdicts.bull: Deeply unloved at -21.7% YTD and $1.1B cap with FY26 guidance reaffirmed, so the drawdown has cheapened a name whose renewables/infrastructure product lines retain genuine optionality as non-obvious AI facility components (racking, structural, thermal-adjacent hardware).
verdicts.neutral: The AI-node case remains a speculative repurposing pathway with no design wins or data-center revenue disclosed; current fundamentals are an OmniMax integration story with 3.78x net leverage, which neither confirms nor kills the latent thesis.
verdicts.bear: Elevated leverage from the $1.335B OmniMax deal plus organic weakness in residential and Agtech could consume management bandwidth and capital for years, leaving the AI-facility optionality permanently unexercised while the stock trades on housing cyclicality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed data-center or AI-infrastructure order/partnership (e.g., structural or renewables products into hyperscaler builds), or commentary to that effect at the June 10 Wells Fargo conference.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Leverage (3.78x net) and soft organic residential/Agtech demand force deleveraging-first capital allocation, with no AI-chain evidence ever materializing for this strict screen.
